title: linanthus pungens subsp. pulchriflorus
intro: Linanthus pungens subsp. pulchriflorus, also known as Purple Sage, is a perennial plant species in the phlox family, characterized by its purple flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been employed to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Purple Sage include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in a variety of ways.
